2604.06005.title: Disentangling MLP Neuron Weights in Vocabulary Space
2604.06005.paper_summary: Interpreting the information encoded in model weights remains a fundamental challenge in mechanistic interpretability. In this work, we introduce ROTATE (Rotation-Optimized Token Alignment in weighT spacE), a data-free method requiring no forward passes that disentangles MLP neurons directly in weight space. Our approach relies on a key statistical observation: neurons that encode coherent, monosemantic concepts exhibit high kurtosis when projected onto the model's vocabulary. By optimizing rotations of neuron weights to maximize their vocabulary-space kurtosis, our method recovers sparse, interpretable directions which we name vocabulary channels. Experiments on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct and Gemma-2-2B-it demonstrate that ROTATE consistently recovers vocabulary channels that are faithful to the neuron's behavior. ablating individual channels selectively disables corresponding input activations or the promotion of specific concepts. Moreover, aggregating channel-level descriptions yields comprehensive neuron descriptions that outperform optimized activation-based baselines by 2-3x in head-to-head comparisons. By providing a data-free decomposition of neuron weights, ROTATE offers a scalable, fine-grained building block for interpreting LMs.
2604.06005.paper_summary_zh: 解讀模型權重中編碼的信息仍然是機械解釋性的一個基本挑戰。在這項工作中，我們介紹了ROTATE（在權重空間中進行旋轉優化的標記對齊），這是一種不需要前向傳播的無數據方法，能夠直接在權重空間中解開MLP神經元。我們的方法依賴於一個關鍵的統計觀察：編碼一致、單義概念的神經元在投影到模型的詞彙上時展現出高峰度。通過優化神經元權重的旋轉以最大化其詞彙空間的峰度，我們的方法恢復了稀疏且可解釋的方向，我們稱之為詞彙通道。在Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct和Gemma-2-2B-it上的實驗表明，ROTATE始終能夠恢復忠實於神經元行為的詞彙通道。消融單個通道會選擇性地禁用相應的輸入激活或促進特定概念。此外，聚合通道級別的描述產生了全面的神經元描述，在正面比較中超越了優化的基於激活的基準2-3倍。通過提供神經元權重的無數據分解，ROTATE為解釋語言模型提供了一個可擴展的、細緻的構建塊。
2604.06005.author: Asaf Avrahamy et.al.
2604.06005.authors: Asaf Avrahamy, Yoav Gur-Arieh, Mor Geva
